title: Integrate KD.WebAI NOC v13.0 “Singularity Protocol” into KD Platform (ICP-native)

requirements.text: Implement an ICP-native “NOC” domain in the existing Motoko single-actor canister (backend/main.mo) that provides: (1) AI status query, (2) DAO proposals query + vote update, (3) Treasury status query, (4) ZK solvency proof generation + current root query, (5) Omni-mesh status query, and (6) a “universal launch” state toggle. All new public methods must be reachable via the existing frontend actor/declarations flow (no separate canisters, no REST-only endpoints).

quotes:
Add in kd platform  
KD.WEBAI NOC v13.0 – COMPLETE A–Z PRODUCTION PACKAGE

acceptanceCriteria: A new set of candid-exposed methods exists on the existing backend canister for AI/DAO/Treasury/ZK/Omni-mesh/Universal Launch.
Methods are implemented in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture preserved).
Frontend can call these methods through the generated declarations (no fetch('/api/...') required to make the UI work).

requirements.text: Add persistent, upgrade-safe NOC state in the backend (stable storage) for: AIStatus, TreasuryStatus, DAO proposal list + vote counts, ZK current root + last proof time, omni-mesh status, and universal launch/black-hole mode flags. If any current in-memory vars would be lost on upgrade, add a conditional backend/migration.mo to preserve/transform state without breaking existing data.

quotes: This document contains the entire source code, configuration, and deployment instructions for the KD.WebAI NOC v13.0 "Singularity Protocol"

acceptanceCriteria: After upgrading the canister, previously set NOC state values (e.g., votes, currentRoot, launch flag) remain intact.
If migration.mo is introduced, it is conditional and does not run when not needed.
Existing app features continue to operate (e.g., existing dashboard user count calls still succeed).

requirements.text: Implement principal-based RBAC and user registry functions in the existing backend to mirror the NOC “Sovereign Core (RBAC)” intent: support an admin principal (configured via initialization), register caller, admin-only addUser/removeUser by Principal, getUserCount, getUser(principal), and isAdmin(principal). These must be additive and must not remove or break existing AccessControl/Users-based functionality already used by the current KD dashboard.

quotes: public shared(msg) func addUser(user: Principal) : async Bool
public shared(msg) func removeUser(user: Principal) : async Bool
public query func getUserCount() : async Nat
public query func isAdmin(p: Principal) : async Bool

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes principal-based methods equivalent to: register(), addUser(Principal), removeUser(Principal), getUserCount(), getUser(Principal), isAdmin(Principal).
Admin-only enforcement is implemented for addUser/removeUser.
Existing name/email-based admin add user flow continues to work as-is (no breaking changes).

requirements.text: Create a new React route under the existing app (e.g., /kd-platform/noc) that renders a NOC dashboard page with cards/sections for: AI Status, Treasury Metrics (including TVL string), DAO Proposals (list + vote buttons), ZK Root display + “Generate Solvency Proof” action, Omni-mesh status indicator, and Universal Launch overlay trigger. All user-facing text must be English.

quotes: Sample API Routes (proxied via frontend): · GET /api/noc/ai · GET /api/noc/dao/proposals · POST /api/noc/dao/vote · GET /api/noc/treasury · GET /api/noc/omni-mesh

acceptanceCriteria: A new page route exists and is reachable from the app router.
The page shows distinct UI sections for AI, Treasury, DAO, ZK, Omni-mesh, and Universal Launch.
All data displayed on the page is loaded via React Query hooks calling the backend actor.
User-facing strings on the new page are written in English.

requirements.text: Add React Query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts to support the NOC dashboard: getAIStatus, getTreasuryStatus, getDaoProposals, voteOnProposal, getZkCurrentRoot, generateZkSolvencyProof, getOmniMeshStatus, and startUniversalLaunch (or equivalent). Hooks must use the existing useActor hook and follow current queryKey/invalidation patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: NOC-related hooks exist in useQueries.ts and compile in TypeScript.
DAO vote mutation invalidates the proposals query so vote totals refresh.
ZK proof generation invalidates/refetches current root so the UI updates after generation.
Hooks handle unauthenticated/unauthorized errors consistently with existing patterns.

requirements.text: Add an admin-only “NOC Admin” section within the NOC page that allows: (1) adding/removing a user by Principal (principal text input), and (2) toggling universal launch / black-hole mode state (if implemented). This section must only render when the caller is an admin as determined by the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: When isAdmin is true, the NOC Admin section is visible; otherwise it is hidden.
Admin can add/remove a Principal and see user count update (or a success message).
Universal launch / black-hole toggle controls are disabled/hidden for non-admin users.

requirements.text: Port the provided NOC UI components into the existing React+Tailwind app as new components (do not use standalone HTML pages): ComplianceShield, InstitutionalConsole, and UniversalLaunchOverlay. Update any unsupported dependencies (e.g., undefined getNOCActor or fetch('/api/...')) to use the existing actor + React Query hooks instead.

quotes: const actor = await getNOCActor(); // defined elsewhere

acceptanceCriteria: Components compile and render inside the current React app.
UniversalLaunchOverlay reads omni-mesh status via backend actor calls (not hardcoded REST fetch).
UniversalLaunchOverlay triggers a backend method for “startUniversalLaunch” (or equivalent) and updates UI state accordingly.

requirements.text: Add a navigation entry to reach the new NOC route from the existing site navigation (desktop and mobile) without modifying any immutable frontend paths. Keep existing KD Platform routes intact.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can navigate to the NOC page from the main navigation on desktop and mobile.
Existing navigation items and KD Platform routes continue to function.

requirements.text: Add project configuration and documentation updates to reflect an ICP-native deployment (dfx-based) rather than Vercel/Node proxy routes: provide/update a README section describing how to deploy the single backend canister and the frontend, and how to verify NOC endpoints using dfx canister call. Do not add Vercel-only deployment steps.
acceptanceCriteria: README includes dfx-based deploy and verification commands for the NOC methods.
No requirement depends on Vercel-proxied /api/noc/* endpoints to function.
The documented verification commands correspond to actual backend methods implemented in this build.

constraints: Backend must remain a single-actor Motoko canister (all logic in backend/main.mo); do not implement a multi-canister architecture.
Only generate backend/migration.mo if needed to preserve existing state across upgrades (conditional migration policy).

Do not edit frontend immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not implement or require WebSockets/Socket.io or any real-time infrastructure beyond polling via React Query timers.
Do not rely on third-party auth providers; only Internet Identity is supported.
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Deploying or maintaining a Vercel production environment and Vercel server-side proxy endpoints (/api/noc/*).
Implementing actual cross-chain functionality, external chain integration, or real token ledger/vault canisters as separate deployed canisters (out of scope given single-actor constraint).
Implementing irreversible controller handover (“black hole mode”) scripts that remove developer control on real mainnet as an automated action inside the app.
